mechanism_narrative: Higher 3-month growth in retail sales signals strengthening household demand and near-term earnings momentum, causing positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days when the RSAFS trend is rising.
failure_condition: Reject if the walk-forward coefficient on 3-month RSAFS growth is nonpositive, or if top-quintile RSAFS growth months do not deliver higher 20-day SPX returns than bottom-quintile months.

mechanism_narrative: A federal funds rate crossing above 2.25% after a prior rise tightens liquidity and raises equity discount rates, causing negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: Reject if triggered observations have positive 20-day SPX returns in more than 60% of cases or if their mean return is not below the unconditional mean return.

mechanism_narrative: When CPI inflation is rising while the federal funds rate is above its 6-month average, inflation pressure forces tighter policy and compresses valuation multiples, causing negative S&P 500 returns over 60 trading days.
candidate_trigger: CPIAUCSL 3-month annualized inflation rises and FEDFUNDS is above its 6-month average
failure_condition: Reject if the CPIAUCSL-by-FEDFUNDS interaction term is nonnegative in walk-forward regressions or if triggered 60-day returns are not below baseline returns.

mechanism_narrative: Payroll expansion combined with unemployment below 4% signals strong income growth without an immediate recession signal, causing positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
candidate_trigger: UNRATE crosses below 4.0% while PAYEMS is above its 3-month average
regime_hint: late-cycle expansion with tight labor markets
failure_condition: Reject if triggered observations do not produce higher average 20-day SPX returns than untriggered observations or if the interaction coefficient is nonpositive.

mechanism_narrative: A rising industrial production trend signals improving real output and operating leverage for cyclical firms, causing positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 60 trading days.
failure_condition: Reject if the 6-month INDPRO trend has a nonpositive rank IC with 60-day SPX returns over any 3-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: Housing starts crossing below 1200 thousand units signals weakening interest-sensitive demand and future construction-linked earnings pressure, causing negative S&P 500 returns over 60 trading days.
failure_condition: Reject if triggered observations have average 60-day SPX returns equal to or above the unconditional average, or if the hit rate for negative returns is below 50%.

mechanism_narrative: Positive real GDP growth combined with a federal funds rate below 3% signals demand expansion under nonrestrictive policy, causing positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 60 trading days.
candidate_trigger: FEDFUNDS below 3.0% and GDPC1 2-quarter change is positive
failure_condition: Reject if the joint condition fails to produce above-baseline 60-day SPX returns or if the GDPC1-by-FEDFUNDS interaction is not positive when FEDFUNDS is below 3%.

mechanism_narrative: CPI inflation crossing below its 12-month pace signals disinflation and reduces expected policy pressure, causing positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 5 trading days.
candidate_trigger: CPIAUCSL 3-month annualized inflation crosses below its 12-month inflation rate
regime_hint: inflation scare or monetary tightening cycle
failure_condition: Reject if triggered disinflation observations do not have positive average 5-day SPX returns or if their average return is below the unconditional 5-day average.
